commit.message:
refactor(logics): remove flat schema, align deploy eos with Ansible standard model (#72)

* feat(inventory): add multi-group flat inventory schema with credential inheritance

Introduce a second inventory format (globals/groups/hosts) auto-detected
alongside the existing Ansible-style schema. Hosts can belong to multiple
groups; connection parameters (credentials, tls_verify, kind, address) are
resolved with deterministic priority: host > first-matching group > globals,
with field-by-field fallback.

## New components

- `ConnectionInventoryLoader`: schema auto-detection, flat + ansible parsing,
  credential resolution. Raises `InvalidInventoryError` on ambiguous schema
  (flat keys mixed with non-flat top-level keys).
- `ConnectionInventory` / `ResolvedHostConnection` / `ResolvedCredentials`:
  immutable value-object models for resolved connection data. Credentials
  are masked in all display paths.

## Credential key aliases supported

- `credentials.username` / `credentials.password`
- `ansible_user` / `ansible_password`
- `address` / `ansible_host`

## Commands updated

- `deploy eos`: uses ConnectionInventoryLoader; hosts without kind=arista_eos
  silently skipped; per-host tls_verify overrides global verify_ssl flag.
- `info`: displays resolved connection table (credentials masked) for flat
  schema; JSON/YAML output includes schema field.
- `validate`: detects flat schema and validates structure + resolution.

## Backward compatibility

Ansible-style inventories are unchanged. The deployer `verify_ssl` parameter
becomes Optional[bool]; None means inherit per-host setting.

commit.message:
Fix orphaned server notifications on fullSync

Two bugs caused notifications to persist on the server after being
dismissed from the phone:

1. The orphan purge in fullSync() used knownServerIds (built from the
   pre-delete local mappings) to skip entries in the server list. This
   meant any deleteNotification call that failed due to a network error
   would never be retried — the ID was in knownServerIds so the orphan
   check silently passed it. Fix: remove the knownServerIds guard and
   delete everything still on the server after clearing local mappings.

2. Race condition: activeNotifications was snapshotted at the start of
   fullSync(), but by the time postSbn() was called a notification could
   already have been dismissed. onNotificationRemoved() would fire during
   the sync but find no mapping (cleared earlier in fullSync) and return
   early — leaving the freshly-posted server entry with nothing to clean
   it up. Fix: re-snapshot activeNotifications immediately before the
   post loop and skip any keys no longer present.

Also removes the unused serverIds variable from pollServerDismissals.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: OneNote section reader — step 1 for RAG indexing (#76)

* feat: add OneNote section reader for RAG content extraction

Extract text from .one (MS-ONESTORE) binary files without requiring
OneNote to be installed or a native binary parser.

- New service: src/services/onenote-reader.ts
  - Hybrid UTF-16BE + ASCII string extraction
  - 'PageTitle' sentinel-based page boundary detection
  - Returns OneNoteSection with per-page titles, dates, and content,
    plus a combined textContent blob for whole-section RAG indexing
  - Noise filter removes font names, GUIDs, XML fragments, and
    OneNote property keys from extracted text

- New IPC handler: onedrive:read-onenote-file
  - Accepts a full file path, validates .one extension
  - Returns { ok, section } or { ok: false, error }

- Types: OneNotePageContent and OneNoteSectionContent added to
  plugins/types.ts; onedriveReadOneNoteFile added to JarvisApi

- Preload: onedriveReadOneNoteFile exposed via contextBridge

- Tests: 19 new tests using fixture .one files from the installed
  @joplin/onenote-converter package (no binary blobs committed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add OneNote folder integration tests

Proves the full group→OneDrive discovery→file scan→content extraction
pipeline using the @joplin/onenote-converter fixture files as a stand-in
OneDrive root.

- Sets up an in-memory DB group named to match a fixture subdirectory
- Runs discoverCustomerFolderForGroup, scanFilesForFolder, listFilesForFolder
- Reads every .one file found via readOneNoteSection
- Logs a formatted section/page summary (visible with --reporter=verbose)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add OneNote section viewer panel in Groups UI

When a OneDrive folder has .one files, each file now shows a 📖 button
in the expanded file list. Clicking it opens a OneNoteSectionPanel to
the right of GroupsPanel showing the extracted content.

- OneNoteSectionPanel: reads the file via onedriveReadOneNoteFile IPC,
  shows section name + page count in the header, then a card per page
  with title (heading), date, and body text (expandable past 320 chars)
- GroupsPanel: accepts onOpenOneNote callback, renders 📓 icon + 📖 button
  for .one files; constructs full path from folderPath + relativePath
- index.tsx: wires up oneNoteFilePath state, passes onOpenOneNote prop,
  renders OneNoteSectionPanel in groups-layout; clears on panel close
- onboarding.css: adds .onenote-section-panel (420px, max-height 80vh)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: read and open .url Internet Shortcut files in file list

OneNote notebooks hosted on SharePoint are represented locally as .url
shortcut files instead of .one files. This adds first-class support:

- New service src/services/url-shortcut.ts: parses the INI-format .url
  file and returns { url, isOneNote, isSharePoint }
- New IPC handler onedrive:read-url-shortcut: reads .url file -> URL
- New IPC handler shell:open-url: opens a validated https:/onenote: URL
  in the system browser/OneNote app
- GroupsPanel: pre-loads .url info when a folder expands; shows
  notebook+globe icon for OneNote links, link icon for other URLs,
  SharePoint badge, and an Open button that launches the URL

Content extraction requires Microsoft Graph API (notebook lives on
SharePoint, not locally synced). The open button is the practical
workaround until Graph API auth is added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use URL hostname parsing instead of substring check for SharePoint

CodeQL flagged incomplete URL substring sanitization: using .includes()
allows spoofing via paths or subdomains (e.g. evil.com/sharepoint.com).

Replace with proper URL parsing: check parsed.hostname equals or ends
with '.sharepoint.com' to safely identify SharePoint-hosted links.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves DirectLink and DirectImage titles (#1150)

* fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves DirectLink and DirectImage titles

When serialising a document with ToMd, link and image titles were
silently dropped.  A Markdown fragment such as

    [text](url "My Title")

would round-trip as

    [text](url)

because the `DirectLink` / `DirectImage` formatSpan cases matched the
title field with a wildcard.

Fix: give DirectLink and DirectImage their own branches so the optional
title can be appended in CommonMark form (  \"title\").

Three new round-trip tests cover:
  - DirectLink with title
  - DirectLink without title (unchanged behaviour)
  - DirectImage with title

All 284 Markdown tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

commit.message:
Add dhcpmon v6 counter CLI (show/clear) and update tests (#26467)

show dhcp_relay ipv6 counters: repurposed to show dhcpmon v6 counters from COUNTERS_DB (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB wrapper)
clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters: repurposed to clear dhcpmon v6 counters via SIGUSR1/SIGUSR2 protocol (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB)
Merged duplicate v4/v6 dhcpmon functions into shared parameterized versions (prefix parameter for table selection)
Fixed v4 clear to include Malformed/Ignored counter types
Added section separators and comments for code organization
Old dhcp6relay commands still available via standalone commands: show dhcp6relay_counters counts / sonic-clear dhcp6relay_counters
Updated unit tests for renamed functions
Breaking Changes
show dhcp_relay ipv6 counters now reads dhcpmon COUNTERS_DB (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB). Old dhcp6relay counters remain accessible via show dhcp6relay_counters counts. dhcpmon v6 ships in the same image, so this command will always have data.

clear dhcp_relay ipv4 counters now also zeros "Malformed" and "Ignored" counter types. This is intentional — dhcpmon writes these types to COUNTERS_DB, and not zeroing them during the SIGUSR2 cache-sync causes stale values to persist after clear.

clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters now clears dhcpmon COUNTERS_DB (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB). Old dhcp6relay clear remains accessible via sonic-clear dhcp6relay_counters.

Design Notes
SIGUSR signal scope: There is one dhcpmon process per VLAN handling both v4 and v6. SIGUSR1/SIGUSR2 pauses all counter writes (both protocols) which is necessary for consistent clear.
Show/clear type asymmetry: show intentionally excludes Malformed/Ignored from display to keep output clean, but clear must zero all types (including Malformed/Ignored) for the SIGUSR cache-sync protocol to work correctly.
Why I did it
sonic-dhcpmon now supports DHCPv6 counters (PR sonic-dhcpmon#62), writing to
DHCPV6_COUNTER_TABLE in COUNTERS_DB. The CLI needs matching show/clear commands
so operators can view and reset these counters, and so the test infrastructure
can validate them.

Work item tracking
Microsoft ADO (number only):
How I did it
sonic-dhcpmon added DHCPv6 counter support (sonic-dhcpmon#62), writing per-interface v6 packet counts to DHCPV6_COUNTER_TABLE in COUNTERS_DB. However, the CLI had no commands to display or clear these v6 counters. The existing show/clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters commands were thin wrappers around dhcp6relay STATE_DB data, which is a separate counter
source with less granularity (no per-interface breakdown, no per-direction TX/RX).

This PR repurposes those commands to show/clear dhcpmon COUNTERS_DB data instead, giving operators consistent v4/v6 counter visibility through a single CLI interface. The old dhcp6relay counters remain accessible via standalone commands (show dhcp6relay_counters counts / sonic-clear dhcp6relay_counters).

How to verify it
Deploy dhcpmon with v6 support on a DUT with DHCPv6 relay configured.
Run show dhcp_relay ipv6 counters — should display per-VLAN/per-interface
DHCPv6 packet counts from COUNTERS_DB.
Run sonic-clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters — should zero all v6 counters.
Run show dhcp6relay_counters counts — should still show dhcp6relay
STATE_DB counters independently.
Existing v4 commands (show/clear dhcp_relay ipv4 counters) unchanged in behavior.
Unit tests: cd dockers/docker-dhcp-relay/cli-plugin-tests && pytest

Implement comprehensive multi-window/multi-tab session support and a
session alerting/notification system for TracePilot.

## Multi-Tab Session Viewing
- Extract session detail logic into per-instance composable
  (useSessionDetail.ts) with reactive() normalization for type parity
- Tab management store with localStorage persistence, max 20 tabs,
  LRU eviction, and drag-to-reorder
- SessionTabStrip with pointer-based drag-and-drop (reliable in
  WebView2), keyboard nav, context menu, middle-click close
- SessionTabContent renders all tabs via v-for + v-show (keep-alive
  cannot distinguish same-component instances)
- Shared SessionDetailPanel component eliminates duplication between
  route-mode and tab-mode views
- Migrate all 14 consumer files to useSessionDetailContext() helper

## Multi-Window (Popup) Support
- Rust open_session_window / close_session_window commands
- ChildApp.vue lightweight viewer shell (no router/sidebar)
- Window role detection (useWindowRole.ts) branches main.ts startup
- Full session ID passed via URL query param (avoids 8-char collisions)
- Main window closes all viewer windows on exit (onCloseRequested)
- Popup sessions tracked in tab store for alert scope awareness
- Inter-window events for popup lifecycle (popup-session-closed)

## Session Alerting & Notifications
- Alert dispatcher with 4 channels: in-app toast, taskbar flash,
  native OS notification (tauri-plugin-notification), Web Audio chime
- Alert watcher monitors session-end, ask_user tool calls, and errors
- Scope-aware filtering: 'monitored' (tabs + route + popups) vs 'all'
- Alert center drawer with history and per-alert actions
- Consolidated settings panel with triggers/delivery/behavior groups
- Config v5 with alerts section (TS + Rust, backward-compatible)
- Notification click-to-focus with registerActionTypes for Windows
- Staggered test alert for verifying all channels

## Navigation & UX
- Sessions home pill in tab strip for quick return to session list
- Sidebar Sessions click properly exits tab view (nav-sessions event)
- Normal click = route navigation, Ctrl+click = open in tab
- Drag tab outside strip to pop out into new window (closes tab)
- v-show for tab/route switching preserves all tab state

## Bug Fixes (from multi-model code reviews)
- Fix useRoute() called outside Vue injection scope in alert polling
- Fix inverted UserAttentionType (Critical=1, Informational=2)
- Fix drag-popout closing tab before window creation succeeds
- Fix seed/poll race condition with seedInFlight guard
- Fix AudioContext suspended in WebView2 (add ctx.resume())
- Fix tabRefs growing unbounded on tab close
- Fix hidden route view continuing auto-refresh behind tab view
- Fix pruneStaleEntries using scoped sessions for dedup cleanup
- Set default alert cooldown to 20 seconds

* fix: hide console windows for background commands on Windows

Add CREATE_NO_WINDOW (0x08000000) creation flag to three Command::new()
calls introduced in the SDK integration that were spawning visible CMD
prompts when run from the .exe:

- bridge/discovery.rs: PowerShell process for UI server detection
- bridge/manager.rs: 'where copilot' for PATH resolution
- commands/tasks.rs: 'tasklist' for process liveness check

These are background queries that should never show a console window.

left unchanged.

* Bug Fixes

* fix(alerts): resolve three bugs preventing alert notifications from firing

Bug 1: useRouter() after await in onMounted silently fails because Vue 3
clears the component instance after async boundaries. inject() returns
undefined, causing TypeError caught by try/catch. Fixed by passing the
Router instance from App.vue's synchronous setup scope as a parameter.

Bug 2: No periodic session list refresh meant the session-end watcher
(which uses { deep: false }) never fired after startup because nothing
replaced the sessions array reference. Added 30s refreshSessions() timer
that starts/stops reactively with alertsEnabled.

Bug 3: Seeding logic marked ALL ask_user calls as already-seen during
first contact with a session, including live pending ones. This prevented
alerts from ever firing for pre-existing ask_user events. Now seeding
correctly marks all existing ask_user as seen (both completed and pending)
so only NEW ask_user events from future turns trigger alerts, avoiding
both missed alerts and duplicate notifications.

Also moved alertInitDone flag to after successful initialization and added
try/catch around initAlertSystem() to prevent silent failures.

* phan: Fix phpdocs Phan can't parse (#37102)

A lot are things like `@returns` instead of `@return`, JS syntax like
`@param {int}`, and punctuation like `@return array.`.

One thing particularly of note is that when we have a doc comment for a
hook, then later in the function a closure without a doc comment, Phan
was thinking the hook doc belonged to the closure (and then complained
that none of the `@param`s matched). We can fix that by adding a doc
comment for the closure itself.

WAF was already using some advanced syntax 👍, but they syntax they used
was incompatible with Phan in a few places:
* Phan doesn't like leading/trailing spaces in array shapes, so do like
  `array{int, int}` instead of `array{ int, int }`.
* A few places they did `array<>` rather than `array{}`.

* coverage: Switch to raw coverage files (#40013)

It turns out that there really aren't any really good generic coverage
reporting tools, the best you can get is something that highlights
covered lines without function/method stats, branch info, or anything
else fancy.

To provide useful coverage output, we're going to look instead at
generating separate coverage dashboards for PHP (PHPUnit) and JS
(jest/istanbul), each generated by those tools so we can get all the
useful info possible. To do that, we'll need all the coverage commands
to generate the tools' raw data formats rather than a lossy format like
clover.

As part of this conversion, we're adding `jest.config.cjs` everywhere so
we can consistently set `collectCoverageFrom`. By default the base
config will include non-test files under `src/`. Various packages need
additional dirs such as `components/`, and a root-level `index.jsx` or
`global.d.ts` seems common too. Then, since we have config files
everywhere, we can move `coverageDirectory` and `coverageReporters` into
the configs too.

* Use batch requests when possible (#45869)

* WooCommerce Analytics: Implement batched pixel requests

Implements request batching for analytics tracking pixels to improve performance by sending multiple tracking events together rather than individually.

Changes:
- Queue tracking pixels during request and send on shutdown hook
- Use WordPress Requests library request_multiple() for parallel execution when available (100ms timeout)
- Fallback to sequential non-blocking wp_remote_get() when batch API unavailable
- Maintains backward compatibility with existing WC_Tracks_Client implementation

Benefits:
- Parallel execution reduces total request time (10 pixels ~100ms vs ~1000ms sequential)
- Non-blocking fallback ensures no impact when batching unavailable
- Minimal overhead with only 2 new class properties

* Add changelog

* Fix build_pixel_url() method call - remove incorrect parameter

* WooCommerce Analytics: Only use batched requests when request_multiple is available

Optimize batching logic to check for request_multiple support before queuing events. When batching is not supported, fall back to the original implementation by sending events immediately using WC_Tracks_Client::record_event(). This ensures events are not unnecessarily queued when they cannot be batched, improving reliability on systems without the Requests library batching capability.

* WooCommerce Analytics: Remove unused send_with_individual_requests method

Remove the send_with_individual_requests method as it is no longer needed. With the updated logic, non-batching environments now send events immediately using WC_Tracks_Client::record_event() instead of queuing them.

* Fix type mismatch: send pixel URL directly for non-batching fallback

Replace WC_Tracks_Client::record_event() call with direct wp_remote_get() to send the pixel URL. WC_Tracks_Client::record_event() expects an array parameter but we have an event object, and we've already built the pixel URL. This matches the original implementation approach of sending pixel URLs via HTTP GET requests.

* Use WC_Tracks_Client::record_event for non-batching fallback

Simplify the non-batching fallback to use WC_Tracks_Client::record_event() directly, matching the existing pattern used elsewhere in the codebase. Add Phan suppression annotation to handle the type mismatch warning, as WC_Tracks_Client::record_event() accepts both array and event object parameters at runtime.

* Add phpcs ignore comment before Phan suppression

Ensure proper comment formatting by including phpcs ignore before the Phan suppression annotation.

* Fix Phan type mismatch suppression for WC_Tracks_Client::record_event

Corrected the Phan suppression format from @phan-suppress-current-line to @phan-suppress-next-line and used the correct error type PhanTypeMismatchArgument instead of PhanTypeMismatchArgumentProbablyReal. This properly suppresses the type mismatch error when passing WC_Tracks_Event or WC_Analytics_Ch_Event objects to WC_Tracks_Client::record_event() which expects an array.

* Remove error_log call from batch pixel request failure handler

Silently fail without logging when batch pixel requests fail, as tracking pixels should not generate error logs or break the site.

* Use WooCommerce logger instead of error_log for batch pixel failures

Replace error_log with wc_get_logger() to properly log batch pixel request failures through WooCommerce's logging system. This provides better integration with WooCommerce's status tools and maintains consistency with the rest of the WooCommerce ecosystem.

* Update wordpress monorepo (#45915)

* Update wordpress monorepo
* pnpmfile: `@automattic/components` is not compatible with `@wordpress/icons` v11
* Pnpmfile hack for `@wordpress/media-utils` and `@wordpress/admin-ui` missing deps on `react`
* tests: Fix deep mocking
  Various packages are now using `exports`, which means we can't have jest
  mock some deep entry point anymore. Fortunately all the tests seem ok
  with using a Proxy object to mock the whole package.
* pnpm: Hack packages to flag ESM files correctly as ESM
  We do this by injecting a stub `package.json` into the directory holding
  the ESM `.js` files, because the resolution algorithm looks at the
  closest package.json rather than the package-as-a-whole's package.json.

  On the down side, we have to make sure to copy the `sideEffects`
  property into the stub, because that too looks at the closest one.
  Hopefully there aren't more things like that.

  In addition to the Gutenberg packages, the `uuid` package also has this
  issue showing up now for some reason.
* webpack: Set `resolve.fullySpecified` to false for Gutenberg packages
  When the file is correctly flagged as containing ESM, Webpack by default
  requires the full file import rather than a directory or extensionless
  import. We can change that behavior by setting `resolve.fullySpecified`.

  Gutenberg itself doesn't run into this because they're relying on
  webpack/babel to be lenient with files that are flagged as CommonJS but
  contain ESM code.
* scan: Revert dataviews test hack from #45551
  No longer necessary after the ESM-fixing hack in this PR.
* Gutenberg seems to have stopped publishing the `.native.js` entry
  points. Disable linting of our `.native.[jt]s` files until that is
  resolved.
* Fix typechecks
  We can't deep-import the types directly anymore, so we have to start
  with what we can import and use utility functions and accessors to drill
  down to the types we need.
* forms: Hack around `@wordpress/interactivity` going ESM-only.
  I worked out a hacky way to get the test to pass without being too
  invasive.

  In the long run it would be better to convert `packages/forms` to run
  Jest in ESM mode, i.e.
  `NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-vm-modules jest --config=tests/jest.config.js`.
  But making that happen will be a lot more invasive: probably you'll need
  to set `"type": "module"` in `package.json` too so all the existing
  ESM-containing `.js` files get properly interpreted as ESM, and then
  you'll have to fully specify all the imports (i.e. change `import from
  './subdir'` to `import from './subdir/index.js'` and `import from
  './file'` to `import from './file.js'`), and clean up anything else that
  breaks too.

* WooCommerce Analytics: Use Beacon API for reliable event tracking with fallback (#45891)

* Analytics: Use Beacon API for reliable event tracking with keepalive fallback

Implements navigator.sendBeacon() as the primary method for sending analytics events, with graceful fallback to fetch() with keepalive option. This ensures reliable event delivery, especially during page unload scenarios where traditional async requests may be cancelled by the browser.

Changes:
- Add sendEventsViaBeacon() method using navigator.sendBeacon API
- Replace @wordpress/api-fetch with native fetch() + keepalive option
- Pass REST API endpoint URL from server to handle plain permalinks correctly
- Add trackEndpoint to wcAnalytics config in PHP (using rest_url())
- Remove @wordpress/api-fetch dependency from package.json
- Remove unused API_NAMESPACE and API_ENDPOINT constants

Benefits:
- Guaranteed event delivery on page unload (browser queues beacon requests)
- Better mobile browser support (especially iOS Safari)
- Works correctly with plain permalinks
- Filters out sophisticated bots that disable beaconing APIs
- Simpler codebase with native Web APIs

* Replace `jest-environment-jsdom` with `@jest/environment-jsdom-abstract` (#46558)

Jest has introduced a package `@jest/environment-jsdom-abstract` that
can use a specified version of `jsdom` instead of being stuck with the
one it's bundled with. Let's use that.

Most of our projects were already using a customized environment anyway,
to work around jsdom not having `structuredClone`. Although most of
these still had an unused `jest-environment-jsdom` dep, probably from
before that was added.

A few for some reason weren't; those have been fixed (and redundant
`@jest-environment jsdom` comments have been removed).

Then have pnpm reject attempts to re-install `jest-environment-jsdom`,
so people don't accidentally re-add it.

* Oops, fix stray `=` in changelog entries from #46558 (#46572)

* Add tablet device detection to WooCommerce Analytics events (#46631)

* Add tablet device detection to WooCommerce Analytics events

Uses Jetpack Device Detection package to distinguish between mobile phones,
tablets, and desktop devices in the 'device' event property. Previously,
wp_is_mobile() was used which grouped tablets with mobile phones.

The device property now reports:
- 'mobile' for phones
- 'tablet' for tablets
- 'desktop' for desktop/laptop browsers

commit.message:
feat: real tool loop in AgentHarness::run() (Phase 1b)

AgentHarness::run() now implements a proper agent loop:
- Calls LLM via provider.chat() (non-streaming, gets tool_calls)
- Checks stop_reason: EndTurn → return text, ToolUse → execute tools
- Executes tools via ToolRegistry, feeds results back to history
- Loops until final text response or max_tool_rounds exhausted
- LoopDetector: detects repeated tool calls with same args (threshold 3)
- Circuit breaker: 5 consecutive tool failures → stop with explanation
- CancellationToken checked at each iteration
- TokenBudget warnings emitted at 70%/85%/95%
- Events: tool_call_start, tool_result_end, text_delta, turn_complete
- Tool specs from ToolRegistry injected into ChatRequest.tools
- Proper tool result → history message with tool_use_id
- All 1,079 tests pass (6 test mocks updated for chat() vs stream())
